content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/dcambria/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.7/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Why subagents:** You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Model Selection

Use the least powerful model that can handle each role to conserve cost and increase speed.

**Mechanical implementation tasks** (isolated functions, clear specs, 1-2 files): use a fast, cheap model. Most implementation tasks are mechanical when the plan is well-specified.

**Integration and judgment tasks** (multi-file coordination, pattern matching, debugging): use a standard model.

**Architecture, design, and review tasks**: use the most capable available model.

**Task complexity signals:**
- Touches 1-2 files with a complete spec → cheap model
- Touches multiple files with integration concerns → standard model
- Requires design judgment or broad codebase understanding → most capable model

## Handling Implementer Status

Implementer subagents report one of four statuses. Handle each appropriately:

**DONE:** Proceed to spec compliance review.

**DONE_WITH_CONCERNS:** The implementer completed the work but flagged doubts. Read the concerns before proceeding. If the concerns are about correctness or scope, address them before review. If they're observations (e.g., "this file is getting large"), note them and proceed to review.

**NEEDS_CONTEXT:** The implementer needs information that wasn't provided. Provide the missing context and re-dispatch.

**BLOCKED:** The implementer cannot complete the task. Assess the blocker:
1. If it's a context problem, provide more context and re-dispatch with the same model
2. If the task requires more reasoning, re-dispatch with a more capable model
3. If the task is too large, break it into smaller pieces
4. If the plan itself is wrong, escalate to the human

**Never** ignore an escalation or force the same model to retry without changes. If the implementer said it's stuck, something needs to change.

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/superpowers/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution
Vou seguir o workflow. Primeiro: estamos em main, e o plano modifica scripts compartilhados de outros sites/EC2. Preciso pausar antes de começar.

content.text:
Estamos em **main** com mudanças não commitadas em arquivos que o plano também vai modificar (`03-nginx-sites.sh`, `02-php.sh`). Vou pausar antes de continuar.

# ⚠️ Bloqueio antes de iniciar a execução

**Problema 1 — Branch `main`:** A regra do skill é não iniciar implementação em main sem consentimento explícito.

**Problema 2 — Working tree sujo:** Há mudanças não commitadas em arquivos que o plano vai tocar:
- `ec2-deploy/post-deploy/03-nginx-sites.sh` (Task 17 vai modificar)
- `ec2-deploy/post-deploy/02-php.sh` (relacionado)
- `ec2-deploy/blue-green-automation/phase7-cutover.sh` + CHANGELOG
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-webp-automation-design.md` (esta sessão)

Misturar essas mudanças com o trabalho do plano vai gerar commits confusos e dificultar revisão.

**Problema 3 — Repos cruzados:** O plano também escreve em:
- `docker-dev/sites/concertacao/` (repo separado deste — `git status` mostra `Shell cwd was reset` ao mudar de pasta)
- `~/.claude/projects/.../memory/` (memória do user)

Cada repo tem seu próprio histórico. Tasks 12, 23-26 atravessam fronteiras.

## Opções

**(A) Worktree isolado** (recomendado pela skill): cria branch + worktree separado, executa tudo lá, merge no fim. Limpo, mas mais setup.

**(B) Branch nova em main, mantendo working tree atual:** `git stash` → `git switch -c feat/webp-avif-automation` → `git stash pop`. Mais simples, separa o trabalho do que está em main.

**(C) Continuar em main com working tree atual:** Risco de misturar tudo. Não recomendo.

**(D) Commitar/stashear o que está pendente primeiro,** depois decidir A ou B.

## Pergunta

Qual prefere? Sugiro **(D) commit das mudanças pendentes em main** (parecem prontas — phase7, CHANGELOG, 02-php, 03-nginx) e **depois (B) nova branch `feat/webp-avif-automation`** pra trabalho do plano. Ou prefere worktree (A)?

E: confirma que devo seguir tocando os 3 repos diferentes (server-tools v2, site concertacao, memory) ao longo das tasks, ou prefere que eu pare antes de cada cruzamento?

+## [1.6.4] - 2026-05-02
+
+### Fixed
+
+- `phase7-cutover.sh` (1.6.3 -> 1.6.4): hardening pos-auditoria C3.3 do step
+  `cutover_step_1e_sync_uploads_to_fs()`. Corrige dois defeitos materiais:
+  - **Filtro de extensoes ampliado**: incluiu `*.pdf`, `*.zip`, `*.mp3`,
+    `*.mp4`, `*.woff`, `*.woff2`, `*.ttf`, `*.otf` alem das imagens ja
+    cobertas (svg/png/jpg/jpeg/webp/gif). Concertacao tem publicacoes para
+    download (PDFs, ZIPs) e fontes/audios servidos do S3; sem esses tipos,
+    apos o cutover o FS local da green nao tinha esses arquivos e os links
+    de download podiam retornar 404 ate o CloudFront cair de volta para
+    origin. Agora o sync cobre todos os assets servidos diretos.
+  - **Telemetria de `uploads_fs_sync_count` corrigida**: trocou
+    `--only-show-errors` por `--no-progress` no `aws s3 sync`. O flag
+    `--only-show-errors` suprimia as linhas `download:` que o
+    `grep -c '^download:'` precisa contar — resultado: contador sempre
+    zero, telemetria mentindo. Com `--no-progress`, as barras de progresso
+    por chunk sao suprimidas mas as linhas `download:` (uma por arquivo
+    baixado) sao preservadas. Contador agora reflete realidade.
+
+### Why
+
+- Auditor C3.3 identificou que (a) o filtro restrito a imagens deixaria
+  PDFs e ZIPs de publicacoes da Concertacao fora do FS local pos-cutover,
+  e (b) `--only-show-errors` quebrava a contagem de arquivos baixados,
+  invalidando o estado em `cutover.uploads_fs_sync_count` para auditoria
+  pos-cutover.
+
+---
+

diff --git a/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/02-php.sh b/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/02-php.sh
index 1dc8986d1..5d47ff73e 100755
--- a/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/02-php.sh
+++ b/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/02-php.sh
@@ -1,6 +1,13 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
-# Versão: 1.0.2
+# Versão: 1.0.3
 # Changelog:
+#   1.0.3 (2026-05-02) — request_terminate_timeout 120s → 60s default:
+#                        * Auditor C3.4 identificou: 120s permite 20 workers
+#                          presos por Aurora lento acumular ate saturar pool.
+#                          60s e suficiente para requests legitimas (TTFB
+#                          tipico < 5s) e limita acúmulo.
+#                        * Override via FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_${ENV} (consistente
+#                          com max_children).
 #   1.0.2 (2026-05-01) — Hardening: reaplica clamp [5..60] APÓS override de
 #                        FPM_MAX_CHILDREN_${ENV} (defesa contra typos no .env,
 #                        ex: "500" vazaria sem clamp). Adiciona warning quando
@@ -47,6 +54,9 @@ VARIÁVEIS DE AMBIENTE (.env):
   FPM_START_SERVERS_\${ENV}  (opcional) Override de pm.start_servers.
                             Se ausente: derivado de max_children/3.
                             Exemplo: FPM_START_SERVERS_PROD="5"
+  FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_\${ENV} (opcional) Override de request_terminate_timeout
+                            (segundos). Default: 60s. Aceita inteiro ≥ 1.
+                            Exemplo: FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_PROD="60"
 
   Quando os overrides estão presentes, o cálculo dinâmico (vCPUs*5,
   RAM/200MB) é ignorado para o parâmetro correspondente. Útil para
@@ -364,6 +374,24 @@ if (( FPM_START_SERVERS > FPM_MAX_SPARE )); then
 fi
 echo "[INFO] RAM: ${TOTAL_RAM_MB}MB → FPM max_children=${FPM_MAX_CHILDREN} (start=${FPM_START_SERVERS}, min_spare=${FPM_MIN_SPARE}, max_spare=${FPM_MAX_SPARE})"
 
+# request_terminate_timeout: tempo max que um worker FPM pode ficar bloqueado
+# antes de ser morto. Default: 60s (Auditor C3.4: 120s permitia Aurora lento
+# acumular workers presos ate saturar o pool; 60s cobre TTFB legitimo < 5s
+# e limita acúmulo). Override via FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_${ENV}.
+FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT=60
+if [[ -n "${_fpm_env_upper}" ]]; then
+    _fpm_rt_var="FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_${_fpm_env_upper}"
+    _fpm_rt_override="${!_fpm_rt_var:-}"
+    if [[ -n "${_fpm_rt_override}" ]]; then
+        if [[ "${_fpm_rt_override}" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
+            echo "[INFO] FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT override via .env (${_fpm_rt_var}): ${_fpm_rt_override}s (substituindo default=${FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT}s)"
+            FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT="${_fpm_rt_override}"
+        else
+            echo "[WARN] FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT override via .env (${_fpm_rt_var}='${_fpm_rt_override}') inválido (esperado inteiro positivo); usando default=${FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT}s."
+        fi
+    fi
+fi
+
 cat >/etc/php/${PHP_VERSION}/fpm/pool.d/wordpress.conf <<EOF
 ; WordPress PHP-FPM Pool
 [wordpress]
@@ -383,9 +411,11 @@ pm.min_spare_servers = ${FPM_MIN_SPARE}
 pm.max_spare_servers = ${FPM_MAX_SPARE}
 pm.max_requests = 500
 
-; Mata workers presos após 120s — última linha de defesa (nginx fastcgi_read_timeout é 60s)
-; Valor alto para não matar jet_download de PDFs grandes em conexões lentas (12MB @ 3Mbps = ~32s)
-request_terminate_timeout = 120s
+; Mata workers presos após \${FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT}s — última linha de defesa.
+; Auditor C3.4 (2026-05-02): default reduzido de 120s → 60s. Valor alto permitia
+; Aurora lento acumular ate 20 workers presos e saturar o pool. 60s cobre TTFB
+; legitimo (< 5s) com folga e limita acúmulo. Override via FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_\${ENV}.
+request_terminate_timeout = ${FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT}s
 
 ; Security
 security.limit_extensions = .php

content.content:
diff --git a/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/03-nginx-sites.sh b/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/03-nginx-sites.sh
index d56d68a1e..488b6944e 100755
--- a/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/03-nginx-sites.sh
+++ b/ec2-deploy/post-deploy/03-nginx-sites.sh
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
 # ==============================================================================
 # Nginx Site Configuration Script - Single Environment with Cache Control
 # Autor: Daniel Cambría + Warp
-# Versao: 1.12.1
+# Versao: 1.12.2
 #
 # Configura site Nginx com suporte completo a SSL via Load Balancer
 # para o ambiente especificado em ENVIRONMENT (prod|hml|dev)
@@ -37,6 +37,13 @@
 #          (donut/bubble em /sobre-nos/) injeta CSS de gstatic.com/charts/51/css/*
 #          via loader.js; sem isso o tooltip.css/util.css era bloqueado e o donut
 #          nao renderizava.
+# v1.12.2: Bloqueio cirurgico do crawler meta-externalagent (Facebook/Meta).
+#          Incidente 2026-05-01: 631 reqs/poucas horas saturaram FPM, 357 com
+#          status 499 (clientes desconectaram). UA ignora robots.txt em URLs
+#          /?jet_download e /eventos com tribe-bar-date. Solucao: map combina
+#          UA + path; retorna 429 para todo HTML (poupa FPM) mas mantem
+#          /wp-content/uploads/ liberado (Facebook precisa para card preview
+#          legitimo via UA facebookexternalhit, que NAO e bloqueado).
 # ==============================================================================
 set -euo pipefail
 
@@ -223,6 +230,32 @@ else
     log_info "Rate limiting zones já existem. Nenhuma alteração necessária."
 fi
 
+# ==============================================================================
+# Meta crawler block (meta-externalagent) — incidente 2026-05-01
+# ==============================================================================
+# meta-externalagent (Facebook/Meta) ignora robots.txt em /?jet_download e
+# /eventos/...?tribe-bar-date=*, saturou FPM (631 reqs / 357 com 499 em horas).
+# Solucao: bloqueia HTML mas libera /wp-content/uploads/ para preview legitimo
+# de cards via UA facebookexternalhit (NAO bloqueado).
+if ! grep -q '\$deny_meta_html' /etc/nginx/nginx.conf; then
+    log_info "Adicionando bloqueio meta-externalagent ao nginx.conf..."
+    cat > /tmp/nginx_meta_block.tmp << 'METAEOF'
+
+    # ==== Meta crawler block (meta-externalagent) ====
+    # Combina UA + path: 1 = bloquear (HTML), 0 = liberar (uploads, outros UAs)
+    # Regex (?!/wp-content/uploads/) = path NAO comeca com /wp-content/uploads/
+    map "$http_user_agent::$request_uri" $deny_meta_html {
+        default 0;
+        "~*meta-externalagent.*::(?!/wp-content/uploads/)" 1;
+    }
+METAEOF
+    sed -i '/^http {$/r /tmp/nginx_meta_block.tmp' /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
+    rm -f /tmp/nginx_meta_block.tmp
+    log_success "Bloqueio meta-externalagent adicionado."
+else
+    log_info "Bloqueio meta-externalagent já existe. Nenhuma alteração necessária."
+fi
+
 # ==============================================================================
 # WP ROCKET NGINX BYPASS — serve HTML cacheado sem PHP
 # ==============================================================================
@@ -413,6 +446,9 @@ server {
     # WordPress permalinks + WP Rocket bypass
     # Ordem: arquivo exato → cache raiz → cache página → diretório → PHP
     location / {
+        # Bloqueio cirurgico meta-externalagent (incidente 2026-05-01)
+        # Map combina UA + path; uploads ficam liberados para card preview
+        if (\$deny_meta_html = 1) { return 429; }
         limit_req zone=cf_crawlers burst=5 nodelay;
         try_files \$uri \$rocket_root_cache \$rocket_cache_file \$uri/ /index.php?\$args;
     }

content.text:
Tudo claro. As mudanças pendentes são:
- **02-php (1.0.3) + Auditor C3.4**: timeout FPM
- **03-nginx (1.12.2) + Meta crawler block**: incidente 2026-05-01
- **phase7-cutover (1.6.4) + Auditor C3.3**: filtro de extensões + telemetria

Já há memória sobre meta-crawler block (`feedback_meta_crawler_block.md`). Vou agrupar em **2 commits** e o spec/plan webp ficam num **3º commit já na branch nova**.

fix(post-deploy): meta-crawler block + FPM request_terminate_timeout

03-nginx-sites.sh v1.12.1 -> v1.12.2: bloqueio cirurgico meta-externalagent
(incidente 2026-05-01: 631 reqs / 357 com 499). Map combina UA + path,
retorna 429 para HTML mas libera /wp-content/uploads/ para card preview
via facebookexternalhit (UA distinto, nao bloqueado).

02-php.sh v1.0.2 -> v1.0.3: request_terminate_timeout 120s -> 60s default
(Auditor C3.4). 120s permitia Aurora lento acumular workers presos ate
saturar pool; 60s cobre TTFB legitimo (<5s) e limita acumulo. Override
via FPM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_${ENV}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- Filtro de extensoes ampliado: alem de imagens (svg/png/jpg/jpeg/webp/gif),
  inclui pdf/zip/mp3/mp4/woff/woff2/ttf/otf. Concertacao serve publicacoes
  (PDFs, ZIPs) e fontes/audios direto do S3; sem esses tipos no FS local
  da green pos-cutover, links de download retornavam 404 ate CloudFront
  cair de volta para origin.

- Telemetria uploads_fs_sync_count corrigida: troca --only-show-errors
  por --no-progress no aws s3 sync. --only-show-errors suprimia as linhas
  "download:" que o grep -c precisa contar; resultado: contador sempre
  zero, telemetria mentindo. Com --no-progress, barras de progresso por
  chunk sao suprimidas mas as linhas "download:" sao preservadas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

10

12

18

20	
21	    If you have questions about:
22	    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
23	    - The approach or implementation strategy
24	    - Dependencies or assumptions
25	    - Anything unclear in the task description
26	
27	    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.
28	
29	    ## Your Job
30	
31	    Once you're clear on requirements:
32	    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
33	    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
34	    3. Verify implementation works
35	    4. Commit your work
36	    5. Self-review (see below)
37	    6. Report back
38	
39	    Work from: [directory]
40	
41	    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
42	    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.
43	
44	    ## Code Organization
45	
46	    You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more
47	    reliable when files are focused. Keep this in mind:
48	    - Follow the file structure defined in the plan
49	    - Each file should have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface
50	    - If a file you're creating is growing beyond the plan's intent, stop and report
51	      it as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS — don't split files on your own without plan guidance
52	    - If an existing file you're modifying is already large or tangled, work carefully
53	      and note it as a concern in your report
54	    - In existing codebases, follow established patterns. Improve code you're touching
55	      the way a good developer would, but don't restructure things outside your task.
56	
57	    ## When You're in Over Your Head
58	
59	    It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me." Bad work is worse than
60	    no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.
61	
62	    **STOP and escalate when:**
63	    - The task requires architectural decisions with multiple valid approaches

file.content:
# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Code Organization

    You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more
    reliable when files are focused. Keep this in mind:
    - Follow the file structure defined in the plan
    - Each file should have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface
    - If a file you're creating is growing beyond the plan's intent, stop and report
      it as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS — don't split files on your own without plan guidance
    - If an existing file you're modifying is already large or tangled, work carefully
      and note it as a concern in your report
    - In existing codebases, follow established patterns. Improve code you're touching
      the way a good developer would, but don't restructure things outside your task.

    ## When You're in Over Your Head

    It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me." Bad work is worse than
    no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.

    **STOP and escalate when:**
    - The task requires architectural decisions with multiple valid approaches
    - You need to understand code beyond what was provided and can't find clarity
    - You feel uncertain about whether your approach is correct
    - The task involves restructuring existing code in ways the plan didn't anticipate
    - You've been reading file after file trying to understand the system without progress

    **How to escalate:** Report back with status BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT. Describe
    specifically what you're stuck on, what you've tried, and what kind of help you need.
    The controller can provide more context, re-dispatch with a more capable model,
    or break the task into smaller pieces.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
    - What you implemented (or what you attempted, if blocked)
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns

    Use DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if you completed the work but have doubts about correctness.
    Use BLOCKED if you cannot complete the task. Use NEEDS_CONTEXT if you need
    information that wasn't provided. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.
```

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 2 of the WebP+AVIF automation plan.

Branch: `feat/webp-avif-automation` in `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/`. Already on this branch.

## Task Description

Create the file `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh` — the skeleton of the engine. ONLY this task: header informativo, readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC, ANSI inline colors, paginated --help, args parsing limited to detecting --help. The actual work (parsing other args, conversion, parallelism) comes in later tasks 3-7.

Create the file with EXACTLY this content:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ==============================================================================
# Generate WebP + AVIF
# Autor: Daniel Cambría
# Data: 2026-05-02
# Versão: 1.0.0
# DEPENDS_ON: cwebp (libwebp), avifenc (libavif-bin), identify (ImageMagick)
#
# Descrição:
#   Script standalone que gera derivados .webp e .avif para todos os arquivos
#   raster (JPG/JPEG/PNG) sob --target. Idempotente via mtime: regenera apenas
#   quando raster é mais novo que derivado. Usa xargs -0 -P para paralelismo.
#   Em PNG, detecta canal alpha (identify) e escolhe lossless/lossy.
#
# Uso:
#   generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opts]
#
# Variáveis de ambiente:
#   FG_GREEN, FG_RED, ENDC — overrides de cor ANSI
#
# Notas: shellcheck zero warnings
# ==============================================================================

set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'

readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC="Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados para uploads"
readonly SCRIPT_VERSION="1.0.0"

# CORES ANSI (Bureau IT Palette) — inline standalone
: "${FG_GREEN:=\033[38;2;0;255;0m}"
: "${FG_RED:=\033[38;2;255;0;0m}"
: "${FG_ORANGE:=\033[38;2;255;165;0m}"
: "${FG_BLUE:=\033[38;2;0;150;255m}"
: "${FG_GRAY:=\033[38;2;128;128;128m}"
: "${BOLD:=\033[1m}"
: "${ENDC:=\033[0m}"

show_help() {
    cat <<'EOF'
================================================================================
generate-webp.sh — Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados
================================================================================

USO:
  generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opções]

OPÇÕES:
  --target=PATH         Diretório raiz para varrer (obrigatório)
  --quality=N           Qualidade JPG→WebP (default: 80)
  --avif-quality=N      Qualidade JPG→AVIF — min/max range (default: 30/35)
  --parallel=N          Workers paralelos (default: min(8, nproc))
  --formats=LIST        webp,avif (default: ambos)
  --dry-run             Apenas inventário, não converte
  --force               Re-gera mesmo se derivado existe e atualizado
  --logfile=PATH        Arquivo de log (default: stdout)
  --max-fail-pct=N      Threshold de falhas (default: 5 — exit 1 se exceder)
  --max-input-mb=N      Pula rasters acima desse tamanho (default: 30)
  --as-user=USER        Roda binários como USER via sudo (default: detect)
  --help, -h            Esta ajuda

EXIT CODES:
  0  Sucesso (falhas <= --max-fail-pct)
  1  Erro fatal ou falhas > threshold
  2  --help ou --dry-run terminados

EXEMPLOS:
  generate-webp.sh --target=/var/www/html/wp-content/uploads
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --dry-run
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --formats=webp --force
================================================================================
EOF
}

show_help_paginated() {
    if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
        if command -v less >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | less -R
            return
        fi
        if command -v more >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | more
            return
        fi
    fi
    show_help
}

# Args parsing inicial — só detecta --help
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --help|-h)
            show_help_paginated
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

echo "TODO: implementação completa em tasks 3-7"
exit 0
```

## Validation steps

After creating the file:

1. `chmod +x` the file.
2. Validate UTF-8: `file /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh` — expect output containing "UTF-8 Unicode text".
3. Run shellcheck (zero warnings required): `shellcheck /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh`. If shellcheck reports anything, fix it.
4. Test --help: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh --help`. Should display the help text (paginated through less if interactive, otherwise plain).
5. Test no-args: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh` — should print "TODO" and exit 0.

## Commit

After validation passes, commit:

```bash
cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2
git add docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'COMMIT'
feat(webp): scaffold generate-webp.sh with header and --help

Skeleton standalone com header informativo, cores ANSI inline,
e --help paginado (less com fallback more/cat). Implementacao
completa de parsing/conversao vem em tasks 3-7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
COMMIT
)"
```

## Context

The full plan is at `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-webp-avif-automation.md` (Task 2 specifically). The full spec is at `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-webp-automation-design.md`. Pattern reference (standalone script with inline ANSI colors): `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/wp-listing-debug.sh`.

This is task 2 of 28. Tasks 3-7 will progressively add args parsing, pre-checks, inventory, conversion, and parallel runner. Don't anticipate those — keep this skeleton minimal as specified.

## Self-review

Before reporting back:
- Header has Autor, Versão 1.0.0, DEPENDS_ON?
- ANSI colors inline (not sourced)?
- shellcheck zero warnings?
- File is UTF-8?
- chmod +x done?
- Commit message follows convention (Co-Authored-By line)?

## Report format

Report back with status DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT plus what you did, validation results, and any concerns.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ==============================================================================
# Generate WebP + AVIF
# Autor: Daniel Cambría
# Data: 2026-05-02
# Versão: 1.0.0
# DEPENDS_ON: cwebp (libwebp), avifenc (libavif-bin), identify (ImageMagick)
#
# Descrição:
#   Script standalone que gera derivados .webp e .avif para todos os arquivos
#   raster (JPG/JPEG/PNG) sob --target. Idempotente via mtime: regenera apenas
#   quando raster é mais novo que derivado. Usa xargs -0 -P para paralelismo.
#   Em PNG, detecta canal alpha (identify) e escolhe lossless/lossy.
#
# Uso:
#   generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opts]
#
# Variáveis de ambiente:
#   FG_GREEN, FG_RED, ENDC — overrides de cor ANSI
#
# Notas: shellcheck zero warnings
# ==============================================================================

set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'

# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # usados em tasks 3-7 (logging/--version)
readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC="Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados para uploads"
# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # usados em tasks 3-7 (logging/--version)
readonly SCRIPT_VERSION="1.0.0"

# CORES ANSI (Bureau IT Palette) — inline standalone
: "${FG_GREEN:=\033[38;2;0;255;0m}"
: "${FG_RED:=\033[38;2;255;0;0m}"
: "${FG_ORANGE:=\033[38;2;255;165;0m}"
: "${FG_BLUE:=\033[38;2;0;150;255m}"
: "${FG_GRAY:=\033[38;2;128;128;128m}"
: "${BOLD:=\033[1m}"
: "${ENDC:=\033[0m}"

show_help() {
    cat <<'EOF'
================================================================================
generate-webp.sh — Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados
================================================================================

USO:
  generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opções]

OPÇÕES:
  --target=PATH         Diretório raiz para varrer (obrigatório)
  --quality=N           Qualidade JPG→WebP (default: 80)
  --avif-quality=N      Qualidade JPG→AVIF — min/max range (default: 30/35)
  --parallel=N          Workers paralelos (default: min(8, nproc))
  --formats=LIST        webp,avif (default: ambos)
  --dry-run             Apenas inventário, não converte
  --force               Re-gera mesmo se derivado existe e atualizado
  --logfile=PATH        Arquivo de log (default: stdout)
  --max-fail-pct=N      Threshold de falhas (default: 5 — exit 1 se exceder)
  --max-input-mb=N      Pula rasters acima desse tamanho (default: 30)
  --as-user=USER        Roda binários como USER via sudo (default: detect)
  --help, -h            Esta ajuda

EXIT CODES:
  0  Sucesso (falhas <= --max-fail-pct)
  1  Erro fatal ou falhas > threshold
  2  --help ou --dry-run terminados

EXEMPLOS:
  generate-webp.sh --target=/var/www/html/wp-content/uploads
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --dry-run
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --formats=webp --force
================================================================================
EOF
}

show_help_paginated() {
    if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
        if command -v less >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | less -R
            return
        fi
        if command -v more >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | more
            return
        fi
    fi
    show_help
}

# Args parsing inicial — só detecta --help
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --help|-h)
            show_help_paginated
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

echo "TODO: implementação completa em tasks 3-7"
exit 0

# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - ✅ Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - ❌ Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.

file.content:
# Code Quality Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a code quality reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementation is well-built (clean, tested, maintainable)

**Only dispatch after spec compliance review passes.**

```
Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer):
  Use template at requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md

  WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: [from implementer's report]
  PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task N from [plan-file]
  BASE_SHA: [commit before task]
  HEAD_SHA: [current commit]
  DESCRIPTION: [task summary]
```

**In addition to standard code quality concerns, the reviewer should check:**
- Does each file have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface?
- Are units decomposed so they can be understood and tested independently?
- Is the implementation following the file structure from the plan?
- Did this implementation create new files that are already large, or significantly grow existing files? (Don't flag pre-existing file sizes — focus on what this change contributed.)

**Code reviewer returns:** Strengths, Issues (Critical/Important/Minor), Assessment

content.subject: Task 1: Criar fixtures de teste (3 imagens reais)
content.description: Criar diretório docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures/ com sample.jpg, alpha.png (com canal alpha), noalpha.png (sem alpha) gerados via ImageMagick. Usadas pelas tasks 2-7 e 10. Commit em server-tools v2.

content.subject: Task 2: Esqueleto generate-webp.sh com header + --help paginado
content.description: Criar docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh com header informativo (Autor, Versão 1.0.0, DEPENDS_ON), readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC, cores ANSI inline, show_help_paginated (less com fallback more/cat), parsing inicial só de --help. shellcheck zero warnings, UTF-8.

content.subject: Task 3: Parsing completo de args + validação
content.description: Adicionar parsing de --target, --quality, --avif-quality, --parallel, --formats, --dry-run, --force, --logfile, --max-fail-pct, --max-input-mb, --as-user. Validação: target obrigatório+existente, parallel default min(8,nproc). Smoke tests dos cenários de erro.

content.subject: Task 4: Pre-checks de binários (cwebp, avifenc, identify)
content.description: Função check_binaries que verifica cwebp, avifenc, identify no PATH; aborta com instrução de instalação se algum faltar.

content.subject: Task 5: Inventário (find + skip por mtime + dry-run)
content.description: Função build_worklist usando find com -print0 e -size -${MAX_INPUT_MB}M; para cada raster×fmt, skip se derivado existe e mtime>=raster. Dry-run reporta total raster e pendentes. Test com fixtures.

content.subject: Task 6: Função convert_one (alpha detect, validação, descarte)
content.description: Função png_has_alpha via identify e convert_one que escolhe cmd por fmt+mime+alpha. Roda via sudo -u $AS_USER + nice/ionice quando root. Pós-conversão valida com dwebp/avifdec; descarta se ≥ raster ou corrupto. Export para xargs.

content.subject: Task 7: Runner paralelo + flock + relatório + threshold
content.description: worker_invoke + run_batch com flock /var/lock/generate-webp.lock (fallback /tmp), xargs -0 -P, relatório com OK/Descartados/Falhas, exit 1 se fail_pct > MAX_FAIL_PCT.

content.subject: Task 8: Atualizar wordpress.Dockerfile (webp+avif+imagick)
content.description: Adicionar webp, libavif-bin, jpegoptim, pngquant, imagemagick no apt-get install do Dockerfile. Rebuild container e validar binários disponíveis.

content.subject: Task 9: Wrapper std webp-bulk no docker-dev.sh
content.description: Função cmd_webp_bulk + entry no dispatcher case + brand_help_command em show_help. Faz docker cp do script + docker exec -u www-data com --target/--as-user.

content.subject: Task 10: Validação real conversão DEV uploads/2026
content.description: Rodar bulk em uploads/2026 do site concertacao, medir cobertura WebP/AVIF, validar idempotência (run #2 rápido), browser test (DevTools confirma image/avif).

content.subject: Task 11: Mu-plugin canônico bit-webp-on-upload.php
content.description: Criar docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-webp-on-upload.php com hook wp_generate_attachment_metadata + wp_schedule_single_event + bit_webp_proc_run usando proc_open com array (sem shell). Validação grep que não usa exec/shell_exec/system/passthru. php -l.

content.subject: Task 12: Copiar mu-plugin para site Concertação + ativar
content.description: Cópia para sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/mu-plugins/. Validar wp plugin list --status=must-use. Test de upload via wp media import + verificar derivados gerados pelo cron.

content.subject: Task 13: 08-img-opt-tools.sh + libavif-bin (v1.1.0)
content.description: Adicionar libavif-bin ao apt-get install, atualizar show_help, bump 1.0.0 → 1.1.0. shellcheck zero warnings.

content.subject: Task 14: Criar d5-generate-webp.sh
content.description: Wrapper post-deploy EC2 com header padrão (Autor, Versão 1.0.0, DEPENDS_ON), helpers via load_helpers.sh, detecta WP_ROOT/WEB_USER, pre-check binários, invoca /usr/local/bin/generate-webp.sh com --parallel=4 --as-user=$WEB_USER --max-fail-pct=5 --logfile.

content.subject: Task 15: Hook de instalação generate-webp.sh em /usr/local/bin
content.description: Adicionar bloco final ao 08-img-opt-tools.sh que copia generate-webp.sh do repo para /usr/local/bin/ com chmod 755.

content.subject: Task 16: Criar d6-sync-uploads-s3.sh (no-op condicional)
content.description: Script novo que verifica wp plugin is-active s3-uploads. No-op se inativo. Se ativo, aws s3 sync uploads/ → bucket com --include de jpg/jpeg/png/webp/avif e --size-only.

content.subject: Task 17: nginx EC2 — adicionar AVIF ao try_files
content.description: Modificar 03-nginx-sites.sh: try_files $uri.avif $uri.webp $uri =404 + types {image/avif avif; image/webp webp;}. Bump versão.

content.subject: Task 18: nginx DEV — equivalente AVIF try_files
content.description: Mesmo update do try_files no template nginx-base e/ou nginx do site Concertação. rebuild-config + restart e validar com curl -H 'Accept: image/avif'.

content.subject: Task 19: Lock coordenado em 10-importwpcontent.sh
content.description: Envolver bloco de import em flock /var/lock/generate-webp.lock (fallback /tmp). Bump versão.

content.description: share import wpcontent, std webp-bulk em todo uploads/, rsync DRY-RUN para prod, autorização explícita necessária antes do rsync real com --ignore-existing. Validar coverage final em prod.

content.subject: Task 21: Desinstalar EWWW em DEV

content.subject: Task 22: Desinstalar EWWW em PROD (autorização)

content.subject: Task 24: Update docker-dev/sites/concertacao/CLAUDE.md

content.subject: Task 25: Update ec2-deploy/CLAUDE.md (se existir)
content.description: Verificar existência. Se existe, listar d5-, d6-, e bump 08- na lista de scripts post-deploy.

content.subject: Task 26: Memory feedback_webp_avif_generation

content.subject: Task 27: Validação ponta-a-ponta DEV

**Progresso:** 2/28 tasks completadas (7%). 1 commit de fixtures + 1 commit de skeleton.

**Observações sobre cadência:**
- Cada task com subagent + spec review = ~2 minutos + 2 chamadas de agent caras
- Tasks 1, 8, 13, 17-19, 23-26 são **edits triviais** com código literal no plano — subagent + 2 reviews vira teatro
- Tasks 6, 7, 11 são **lógica real** — subagent + reviews fazem diferença
- Tasks 10, 12, 20, 27, 28 são **runtime/validação** — não rodam fora da minha sessão

**Proposta de ajuste** (ainda dentro do espírito do skill, só cortando teatro):
- **Subagent + spec review + code quality review:** Tasks 6, 7, 11 (lógica complexa)
- **Subagent + spec review:** Tasks 3-5, 9, 14, 16 (lógica média; quality já garantida pelo plano literal)

- **Bloqueio com você:** Tasks 10, 12, 20, 22, 27, 28 (runtime, autorização, validação ponta-a-ponta)

Isso reduz ~50 chamadas de agent pra ~20-25, sem perda de qualidade real.

**Ok aplicar essa cadência ajustada, ou prefere subagent estrito em todas as 28?** Em qualquer caso, sigo agora pra Task 3.

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 3 of the WebP+AVIF automation plan.

Branch: `feat/webp-avif-automation` in `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/`. Already on this branch.

## Task Description

Replace the temporary `for arg in "$@"` block + `echo "TODO"` at the end of `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh` with a complete args parser that handles ALL flags from the spec, plus validation.

## Context

The skeleton from Task 2 currently ends with:

```bash
# Args parsing inicial — só detecta --help
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --help|-h)
            show_help_paginated
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

echo "TODO: implementação completa em tasks 3-7"
exit 0
```

You replace that block with full parsing + validation. The script will still END with a TODO line (because conversion logic comes in tasks 4-7), but parsing should be complete.

## Implementation

Replace from `# Args parsing inicial — só detecta --help` (line ~91) through `exit 0` (line ~102) with:

```bash
# ==============================================================================
# DEFAULTS
# ==============================================================================
TARGET=""
QUALITY=80
AVIF_QMIN=30
AVIF_QMAX=35
PARALLEL=""
FORMATS="webp,avif"
DRY_RUN=false
FORCE=false
LOGFILE=""
MAX_FAIL_PCT=5
MAX_INPUT_MB=30
AS_USER=""

# ==============================================================================
# ARGS PARSING
# ==============================================================================
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --help|-h)
            show_help_paginated
            exit 2
            ;;
        --target=*)        TARGET="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --quality=*)       QUALITY="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --avif-quality=*)  AVIF_QMIN="${arg#*=}"; AVIF_QMAX=$((AVIF_QMIN + 5)) ;;
        --parallel=*)      PARALLEL="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --formats=*)       FORMATS="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --dry-run)         DRY_RUN=true ;;
        --force)           FORCE=true ;;
        --logfile=*)       LOGFILE="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --max-fail-pct=*)  MAX_FAIL_PCT="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --max-input-mb=*)  MAX_INPUT_MB="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --as-user=*)       AS_USER="${arg#*=}" ;;
        *)
            echo -e "${FG_RED}Argumento desconhecido: $arg${ENDC}" >&2
            echo "Use --help para ver opções." >&2
            exit 1
            ;;

# ==============================================================================
# PRE-CHECKS
# ==============================================================================
check_binaries() {
    local missing=()
    local bin
    for bin in cwebp avifenc identify; do
        command -v "$bin" >/dev/null 2>&1 || missing+=("$bin")
    done
    if (( ${#missing[@]} > 0 )); then
        echo -e "${FG_RED}Binários ausentes: ${missing[*]}${ENDC}" >&2
        echo "Instalar (Debian/Ubuntu):" >&2
        echo "  apt-get install -y webp libavif-bin imagemagick" >&2
        echo "Instalar (macOS):" >&2
        echo "  brew install webp libavif imagemagick" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ==============================================================================
# Generate WebP + AVIF
# Autor: Daniel Cambría
# Data: 2026-05-02
# Versão: 1.0.0
# DEPENDS_ON: cwebp (libwebp), avifenc (libavif-bin), identify (ImageMagick)
#
# Descrição:
#   Script standalone que gera derivados .webp e .avif para todos os arquivos
#   raster (JPG/JPEG/PNG) sob --target. Idempotente via mtime: regenera apenas
#   quando raster é mais novo que derivado. Usa xargs -0 -P para paralelismo.
#   Em PNG, detecta canal alpha (identify) e escolhe lossless/lossy.
#
# Uso:
#   generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opts]
#
# Variáveis de ambiente:
#   FG_GREEN, FG_RED, ENDC — overrides de cor ANSI
#
# Notas: shellcheck zero warnings
# ==============================================================================

set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'

# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # usados em tasks 3-7 (logging/--version)
readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC="Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados para uploads"
readonly SCRIPT_VERSION="1.0.0"

# CORES ANSI (Bureau IT Palette) — inline standalone
: "${FG_GREEN:=\033[38;2;0;255;0m}"
: "${FG_RED:=\033[38;2;255;0;0m}"
: "${FG_ORANGE:=\033[38;2;255;165;0m}"
: "${FG_BLUE:=\033[38;2;0;150;255m}"
: "${FG_GRAY:=\033[38;2;128;128;128m}"
: "${BOLD:=\033[1m}"
: "${ENDC:=\033[0m}"

show_help() {
    cat <<'EOF'
================================================================================
generate-webp.sh — Geração de WebP+AVIF derivados
================================================================================

USO:
  generate-webp.sh --target=PATH [opções]

OPÇÕES:
  --target=PATH         Diretório raiz para varrer (obrigatório)
  --quality=N           Qualidade JPG→WebP (default: 80)
  --avif-quality=N      Qualidade JPG→AVIF — min/max range (default: 30/35)
  --parallel=N          Workers paralelos (default: min(8, nproc))
  --formats=LIST        webp,avif (default: ambos)
  --dry-run             Apenas inventário, não converte
  --force               Re-gera mesmo se derivado existe e atualizado
  --logfile=PATH        Arquivo de log (default: stdout)
  --max-fail-pct=N      Threshold de falhas (default: 5 — exit 1 se exceder)
  --max-input-mb=N      Pula rasters acima desse tamanho (default: 30)
  --as-user=USER        Roda binários como USER via sudo (default: detect)
  --help, -h            Esta ajuda

EXIT CODES:
  0  Sucesso (falhas <= --max-fail-pct)
  1  Erro fatal ou falhas > threshold
  2  --help ou --dry-run terminados

EXEMPLOS:
  generate-webp.sh --target=/var/www/html/wp-content/uploads
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --dry-run
  generate-webp.sh --target=. --formats=webp --force
================================================================================
EOF
}

show_help_paginated() {
    if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
        if command -v less >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | less -R
            return
        fi
        if command -v more >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            show_help | more
            return
        fi
    fi
    show_help
}

# ==============================================================================
# DEFAULTS
# ==============================================================================
# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Vars consumidas em tasks 4-7 (conversão/relatório)
TARGET=""
QUALITY=80
AVIF_QMIN=30
AVIF_QMAX=35
PARALLEL=""
FORMATS="webp,avif"
DRY_RUN=false
FORCE=false
LOGFILE=""
MAX_FAIL_PCT=5
MAX_INPUT_MB=30
AS_USER=""

# ==============================================================================
# ARGS PARSING
# ==============================================================================
# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Vars consumidas em tasks 4-7 (conversão/relatório)
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --help|-h)
            show_help_paginated
            exit 2
            ;;
        --target=*)        TARGET="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --quality=*)       QUALITY="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --avif-quality=*)  AVIF_QMIN="${arg#*=}"; AVIF_QMAX=$((AVIF_QMIN + 5)) ;;
        --parallel=*)      PARALLEL="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --formats=*)       FORMATS="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --dry-run)         DRY_RUN=true ;;
        --force)           FORCE=true ;;
        --logfile=*)       LOGFILE="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --max-fail-pct=*)  MAX_FAIL_PCT="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --max-input-mb=*)  MAX_INPUT_MB="${arg#*=}" ;;
        --as-user=*)       AS_USER="${arg#*=}" ;;
        *)
            echo -e "${FG_RED}Argumento desconhecido: $arg${ENDC}" >&2
            echo "Use --help para ver opções." >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# ==============================================================================
# VALIDAÇÃO DE ARGS
# ==============================================================================
if [[ -z "$TARGET" ]]; then
    echo -e "${FG_RED}--target=PATH é obrigatório${ENDC}" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -d "$TARGET" ]]; then
    echo -e "${FG_RED}--target não é um diretório existente: $TARGET${ENDC}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Default parallel = min(8, nproc/sysctl)
if [[ -z "$PARALLEL" ]]; then
    nproc_count=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4)
    PARALLEL=$(( nproc_count < 8 ? nproc_count : 8 ))
fi

# Setup logfile (apenas redireciona stderr — stdout permanece para relatório no terminal)
if [[ -n "$LOGFILE" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOGFILE")"
    exec 2> >(tee -a "$LOGFILE" >&2)
fi

echo -e "${FG_BLUE}generate-webp.sh v${SCRIPT_VERSION}${ENDC}"
echo "  target=$TARGET  parallel=$PARALLEL  formats=$FORMATS  dry-run=$DRY_RUN"

echo "TODO: pre-checks/inventário/conversão em tasks 4-7"
exit 0

# Pre-check binários (após validação de args para evitar mensagens redundantes em --help)
check_binaries

# Setup logfile (apenas redireciona stderr — stdout permanece para relatório no terminal)
if [[ -n "$LOGFILE" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOGFILE")"
    exec 2> >(tee -a "$LOGFILE" >&2)
fi

echo -e "${FG_BLUE}generate-webp.sh v${SCRIPT_VERSION}${ENDC}"
echo "  target=$TARGET  parallel=$PARALLEL  formats=$FORMATS  dry-run=$DRY_RUN"

echo "TODO: pre-checks/inventário/conversão em tasks 4-7"
exit 0

content.subject: Task 2: Esqueleto generate-webp.sh com header + --help paginado
content.description: Criar docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh com header informativo (Autor, Versão 1.0.0, DEPENDS_ON), readonly WP_SCRIPT_DESC, cores ANSI inline, show_help_paginated (less com fallback more/cat), parsing inicial só de --help. shellcheck zero warnings, UTF-8.

content.subject: Task 3: Parsing completo de args + validação
content.description: Adicionar parsing de --target, --quality, --avif-quality, --parallel, --formats, --dry-run, --force, --logfile, --max-fail-pct, --max-input-mb, --as-user. Validação: target obrigatório+existente, parallel default min(8,nproc). Smoke tests dos cenários de erro.

content.subject: Task 4: Pre-checks de binários (cwebp, avifenc, identify)
content.description: Função check_binaries que verifica cwebp, avifenc, identify no PATH; aborta com instrução de instalação se algum faltar.

content.subject: Task 5: Inventário (find + skip por mtime + dry-run)
content.description: Função build_worklist usando find com -print0 e -size -${MAX_INPUT_MB}M; para cada raster×fmt, skip se derivado existe e mtime>=raster. Dry-run reporta total raster e pendentes. Test com fixtures.

content.subject: Task 6: Função convert_one (alpha detect, validação, descarte)
content.description: Função png_has_alpha via identify e convert_one que escolhe cmd por fmt+mime+alpha. Roda via sudo -u $AS_USER + nice/ionice quando root. Pós-conversão valida com dwebp/avifdec; descarta se ≥ raster ou corrupto. Export para xargs.

content.subject: Task 7: Runner paralelo + flock + relatório + threshold
content.description: worker_invoke + run_batch com flock /var/lock/generate-webp.lock (fallback /tmp), xargs -0 -P, relatório com OK/Descartados/Falhas, exit 1 se fail_pct > MAX_FAIL_PCT.

content.subject: Task 8: Atualizar wordpress.Dockerfile (webp+avif+imagick)

input.description: Implement Task 5: inventory
input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 5 of the WebP+AVIF automation plan.

Branch: `feat/webp-avif-automation` in `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/`. Already on this branch.

## Task Description

Add to `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh`:

1. A function `build_worklist` that walks `--target` and emits NUL-separated `raster<TAB>fmt` records for every (raster, format) pair that needs conversion.
2. A `--dry-run` short-circuit that prints inventory counts and exits 2.

The runner that consumes the worklist comes in Task 7. For now, dry-run is the only consumer.

## Current state of the script

The script currently has (in order): shebang, header, set -euo pipefail, readonly vars, ANSI colors, `show_help`, `show_help_paginated`, `check_binaries`, DEFAULTS block, ARGS PARSING (handles `--help` and all flags), VALIDAÇÃO DE ARGS (target required + must be dir), parallel default, `check_binaries` invocation, logfile redirect setup, and an echo cabeçalho followed by:

```bash
echo "TODO: pre-checks/inventário/conversão em tasks 4-7"
exit 0
```

Your changes:

1. **Insert `build_worklist` function** between `check_binaries()` definition and `DEFAULTS` block (i.e., as a peer function definition before any code runs at top level). This keeps function definitions grouped at top of script.

2. **Replace the final `echo "TODO" + exit 0`** with a dry-run short-circuit block.

## Implementation

### Function `build_worklist`

```bash
# ==============================================================================
# INVENTÁRIO
# ==============================================================================

# Emite no stdout linhas NUL-separadas no formato "raster<TAB>fmt" para cada
# par (raster, formato) que precisa conversão. Skip por mtime: só enfileira
# se derivado não existe ou está mais velho que o raster.
# Filtros aplicados:
#   - Extensões: jpg/jpeg/png (case-insensitive)
#   - Tamanho: < MAX_INPUT_MB (proteção OOM)
#   - Formatos: cada item de FORMATS (csv → loop)
# Quando FORCE=true, ignora skip por mtime.
build_worklist() {
    local raster fmt outfile
    while IFS= read -r -d '' raster; do
        for fmt in ${FORMATS//,/ }; do
            outfile="${raster}.${fmt}"
            if [[ "$FORCE" != "true" ]] && [[ -f "$outfile" ]]; then
                # Skip se outfile NÃO é mais velho que raster
                # ([[ -nt ]] retorna true se LHS é mais novo OU igual a RHS)
                if [[ ! "$raster" -nt "$outfile" ]]; then
                    continue
                fi
            fi
            printf '%s\t%s\0' "$raster" "$fmt"
        done
    done < <(
        find "$TARGET" -type f \
            \( -iname '*.jpg' -o -iname '*.jpeg' -o -iname '*.png' \) \
            -size "-${MAX_INPUT_MB}M" \
            -print0
    )
}
```

### Dry-run short-circuit

Replace the existing:

```bash
echo "TODO: pre-checks/inventário/conversão em tasks 4-7"
exit 0
```

With:

```bash
# ==============================================================================
# DRY-RUN: apenas inventário
# ==============================================================================
if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
    total_raster=$(find "$TARGET" -type f \
        \( -iname '*.jpg' -o -iname '*.jpeg' -o -iname '*.png' \) \
        -size "-${MAX_INPUT_MB}M" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    pending=$(build_worklist | tr -cd '\0' | wc -c | tr -d ' ')
    echo
    echo "  Total raster: $total_raster"
    echo "  Pendentes (par raster×fmt): $pending"
    exit 2
fi

echo "TODO: conversão em tasks 6-7"
exit 0
```

## Validation

Fixtures live at `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures/` (sample.jpg, alpha.png, noalpha.png — all small, no derivatives).

1. shellcheck zero warnings:
   ```bash
   shellcheck /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh
   ```

2. Test dry-run on clean fixtures (no derivatives exist):
   ```bash
   cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures
   /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh --target=. --dry-run
   ```
   Expected output: header line, blank line, "Total raster: 3", "Pendentes (par raster×fmt): 6" (3 rasters × 2 formats: webp+avif). Exit code 2.

3. Test idempotência — create a fake `.webp` newer than raster, expect skip:
   ```bash
   cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures
   touch sample.jpg.webp  # mtime now > sample.jpg
   /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh --target=. --dry-run
   rm sample.jpg.webp
   ```
   Expected: "Pendentes: 5" (sample.jpg.webp pulado; outros 5 ainda pendentes).

4. Test --formats restriction:
   ```bash
   cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures
   /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh --target=. --formats=webp --dry-run
   ```
   Expected: "Pendentes: 3" (só webp).

5. Test --force:
   ```bash
   cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/tests/fixtures
   touch sample.jpg.webp
   /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh --target=. --force --dry-run
   rm sample.jpg.webp
   ```
   Expected: "Pendentes: 6" (force ignora mtime check).

6. Sanity: --target ainda valida diretório, --help ainda funciona, args ainda parsam.

## Concerns to handle proactively

- The DEFAULTS block had `# shellcheck disable=SC2034` block-disable for vars consumed in tasks 4-7. After this task, `FORCE`, `FORMATS`, `MAX_INPUT_MB`, `DRY_RUN` are now USED. `QUALITY`, `AVIF_QMIN`, `AVIF_QMAX`, `MAX_FAIL_PCT`, `AS_USER` remain unused (tasks 6-7). The block-level disable is still appropriate; don't change it. (If shellcheck reports specific SC2034 warnings on those names anyway, narrow the disable to a comment per line, but most likely the block disable suffices.)

- `${FORMATS//,/ }` is intentionally unquoted to perform word-splitting. shellcheck SC2086 may or may not flag this; if it does, suppress on that specific line with a justification comment.

- `find ... -size -30M` uses M (megabytes). On busybox find this can vary, but on GNU find and macOS find both interpret M as megabytes; that matches our target environments (Ubuntu containers, macOS dev host).

- `printf '%s\t%s\0'` emits NUL-terminated records — required by xargs -0 in Task 7. Don't use `\n` here.

## Commit

```bash
cd /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2
git add docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'COMMIT'
feat(webp): inventory with mtime skip + dry-run short-circuit

build_worklist emite "raster<TAB>fmt\0" para cada par pendente
(skip se derivado nao mais velho que raster; --force ignora).
Filtros: jpg/jpeg/png case-insensitive, < MAX_INPUT_MB, formatos
de --formats. dry-run reporta total_raster e pendentes, exit 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
COMMIT
)"
```

## Self-review

- shellcheck zero warnings?
- 5 cenários de validação passam?
- printf usa NUL terminator (não \n)?
- `[[ ! "$raster" -nt "$outfile" ]]` é a checagem correta para "skip se outfile já existe e está atualizado"?
- find -size -${N}M cobre o filtro de tamanho?
- Função `build_worklist` aparece antes do código top-level (com as outras definições)?
- Exit code 2 no dry-run (consistente com --help)?

## Report

DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT — diga o que fez, smoke tests reais, files changed, qualquer concern.

11	#   raster (JPG/JPEG/PNG) sob --target. Idempotente via mtime: regenera apenas
12	#   quando raster é mais novo que derivado. Usa xargs -0 -P para paralelismo.
13	#   Em PNG, detecta canal alpha (identify) e escolhe lossless/lossy.
14	#

# ==============================================================================
# INVENTÁRIO
# ==============================================================================

# Emite no stdout linhas NUL-separadas no formato "raster<TAB>fmt" para cada
# par (raster, formato) que precisa conversão. Skip por mtime: só enfileira
# se derivado não existe ou está mais velho que o raster.

#   - Tamanho: < MAX_INPUT_MB (proteção OOM)
#   - Formatos: cada item de FORMATS (csv → loop)
# Quando FORCE=true, ignora skip por mtime.
build_worklist() {
    local raster fmt outfile
    while IFS= read -r -d '' raster; do
        for fmt in ${FORMATS//,/ }; do
            outfile="${raster}.${fmt}"
            if [[ "$FORCE" != "true" ]] && [[ -f "$outfile" ]]; then

```

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 6 of the WebP+AVIF automation plan.

Branch: `feat/webp-avif-automation` in `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/`. Already on this branch.

## Task Description

Add to `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/common/scripts/generate-webp.sh` two functions: `png_has_alpha` and `convert_one`. They will be invoked by xargs workers in Task 7. For now, validate them by calling directly on the test fixtures.

## Current state

The script has all parts from Tasks 2-5. Notable existing context:
- `set -euo pipefail`, `IFS=$'\n\t'` on top
- ANSI colors (FG_RED, FG_GREEN, FG_ORANGE, FG_BLUE, FG_GRAY, BOLD, ENDC) inline
- `check_binaries()` and `build_worklist()` already defined
- Top-level vars: TARGET, QUALITY, AVIF_QMIN, AVIF_QMAX, FORMATS, AS_USER (string, may be empty)
- Final block ends with `echo "TODO: conversão em tasks 6-7"; exit 0` — replace this last echo+exit eventually, but in THIS task we keep it (Task 7 replaces with the parallel runner).

## What you implement

### 1. Function `png_has_alpha`

Detects whether a PNG has an alpha channel via `identify`.

```bash
# Detecta canal alpha em PNG via identify. Sai 0 se tem alpha, 1 se não.
# Usado para escolher cwebp -lossless (alpha) vs cwebp -q (sem alpha).
png_has_alpha() {
    local file="$1"
    local channels
    channels=$(identify -format '%[channels]' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [[ "$channels" == *"a"* ]]
}
```

### 2. Function `convert_one`

Converte um único arquivo (raster, fmt). Trata:
- Detecção de MIME (`file --mime-type -b`) e branch JPG/PNG.
- PNG com alpha → WebP `-lossless`. Sem alpha → WebP `-q $QUALITY`. AVIF lida com alpha nativo (sempre `--min/--max`).
- Quando rodando como root e `AS_USER` setado, executa via `sudo -u "$AS_USER"`. Quando root sem AS_USER, ainda aplica `nice -n 19 ionice -c 3` (defesa I/O).
- Pós-conversão: arquivo existe + tamanho > 0; valida decodificação com `dwebp` ou `avifdec`; descarta se derivado >= raster.
- Exit codes da função: 0 OK, 1 falha de conversão (cwebp/avifenc retornou erro), 2 derivado descartado (>= raster ou corrompido).

```bash
# Converte um único par (raster, fmt). Args: $1=raster $2=fmt
# Returns: 0 ok, 1 falha conversão, 2 derivado descartado.
convert_one() {
    local raster="$1"
    local fmt="$2"
    local outfile="${raster}.${fmt}"
    local mime
    mime=$(file --mime-type -b "$raster" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    local cmd=()

    case "$fmt" in
        webp)
            if [[ "$mime" == "image/png" ]] && png_has_alpha "$raster"; then
                cmd=(cwebp -lossless -metadata none -mt -quiet "$raster" -o "$outfile")
            else
                cmd=(cwebp -q "$QUALITY" -metadata none -mt -quiet "$raster" -o "$outfile")
            fi
            ;;
        avif)
            cmd=(avifenc --min "$AVIF_QMIN" --max "$AVIF_QMAX" --speed 6 --jobs 1 \
                "$raster" "$outfile")
            ;;
        *)
            echo "[SKIP] formato desconhecido: $fmt" >&2
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Permissões + niceness
    if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]] && [[ -n "$AS_USER" ]]; then
        sudo -u "$AS_USER" nice -n 19 ionice -c 3 "${cmd[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
    elif [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
        nice -n 19 ionice -c 3 "${cmd[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
    else
        "${cmd[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
    fi

    # Pós-validação: existe e tamanho > 0
    if [[ ! -s "$outfile" ]]; then
        rm -f "$outfile"
        return 1
    fi

    # Validação de decodificação
    case "$fmt" in
        webp)
            dwebp -quiet "$outfile" -o /dev/null 2>/dev/null || { rm -f "$outfile"; return 2; }
            ;;
        avif)
            avifdec --info "$outfile" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { rm -f "$outfile"; return 2; }
            ;;
    esac

    # Tamanho: se derivado >= raster, descarta (browser cai no raster)
    local rsize osize
    rsize=$(stat -f%z "$raster" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$raster" 2>/dev/null)
    osize=$(stat -f%z "$outfile" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$outfile" 2>/dev/null)
    if (( osize >= rsize )); then
        rm -f "$outfile"
        return 2
    fi

    return 0
}
```

### 3. Export functions for xargs subshells (Task 7 will use)

After both function definitions (still at function-defs section, before DEFAULTS):

```bash
# Exporta funções e vars de configuração para subshells de xargs (Task 7).
export -f png_has_alpha convert_one
export QUALITY AVIF_QMIN AVIF_QMAX AS_USER FG_GREEN FG_RED FG_ORANGE ENDC
```

**Important**: vars in the export list are in DEFAULTS block — but DEFAULTS comes AFTER function definitions in the current script. The `export QUALITY ...` line must run AFTER args parsing has set those vars. So:
- Function definitions go in the "INVENTÁRIO" section area (alongside `build_worklist`).
